source_text: ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT 159 bromine exerted highest inhibition against all the bacterial strains and showed highest efficacy 160 against S. aureus at 12.5 µg/mL of MIC as compared to standards ciprofloxacin (50 µg/mL, Clog 161 P = -0.7252) and chloramphenicol (50 µg/mL, Clog P = 1.293). In addition, compound 5q (Clog T 162 P = 3.9272) with higher lipophilicity flaunted potential inhibitory action against E. coli and S. P 163 pyogenes at MIC of 25 µg/mL. Moreover, the above mentioned derivative 5q displayed excellent I R 164 inhibition against P. aeruginosa at 25 µg/mL of MIC. Furthermore, among the second line active C 165 compounds, 5h (3-Cl, Clog P = 3.7772) and 5r (4-Br, Clog P = 3.9272) indicated MIC of 50 S 166 µg/mL, in which more lipophilic compound 5r showed better results against both E. coli and S. U 167 pyogenes. In addition, the above mentioned derivatives 5h and 5r were also found to N 168 demonstrate excellent efficacy against S. aureus at 25 µg/mL of MIC and analogue 5r also A 169 demonstrated MIC of 50 µg/mL against P. aeruginosa. Derivatives 5e (3-F, Clog P = 3.2072) M 170 with fluoro, 5g (2-Cl, Clog P = 3.7772) with chloro and 5k (3-CH , Clog P = 3.5632) with 3 171 me...

ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT 555 pyogenes MTCC-442, Escherichia coli MTCC-443, Pseudomonas aeruginosa MTCC-1688 and 556 methicillin-resistant S. aureus (MRSA isolate ATCC 43300). Antifungal activity was carried out 557 against the yeast-like pathogenic fungus Candida albicans MTCC 227. All MTCC and ATCC T 558 cultures were collected from Institute of Microbial Technology, Chandigarh.
